subject: Computer Networking
title: Network Interface Cards
description: A quick win: understand Network Interface Cards.

hook.text: Every networked device needs a NIC — the hardware that speaks the network's language.

buildup.text: A NIC frames data into packets, manages transmission timing, and detects errors on the wire.

discovery.text: Every NIC has a unique MAC address — a 48-bit hardware ID burned in at the factory. No two match.

twist.text: Modern NICs are built into motherboards. Dedicated cards now mainly serve servers needing extra speed.

climax.text: Some NICs offload checksums, encryption, or TCP from the CPU to boost performance.

punchline.text: The NIC is your device's passport to the network.

quiz.question: What unique identifier does every NIC have?
options: A MAC address assigned at the factory
An IP address from the ISP
A serial number from the operating system
